title: Roku Q3 2021 Earnings Report
text: Roku reported strong Q3 2021 results with 56.4 million active users and revenue of $680 million, meeting expectations. The company achieved gross profit of $364 million, exceeding the previous quarter's projection of $320 million. The platform generated 18 billion streaming hours during the quarter, recovering slightly from Q2's 17.4 billion hours. This represented a recovery after Q1's peak of 18.3 billion hours. The company attributed the earlier decline to "loosening COVID-19 restrictions," as customers engaged in more outdoor activities. Management projected next quarter revenue would reach $893 million with profit anticipated at $385 million, signaling confidence in upcoming holiday season performance despite supply chain challenges. Roku continued expanding its content library following its Quibi acquisition and achieved Emmy recognition, including a win for J.B. Smoove. The company also released new hardware including refreshed 4K streaming devices and announced a Shopify integration to assist small advertisers. The company faced "ongoing headwinds created by the global supply chain disruptions," according to shareholder communications. Additionally, negotiations with Google regarding YouTube app placement on new Roku devices remained unresolved.
